items.title: TypeScript
items.description: Typed superset of JavaScript. As my most-used language and the bread & butter of my day job, it's my first choice for anything web.

items.title: JavaScript
items.description: Core technology of the web. While I started out with JavaScript, I have since moved on to TypeScript wherever possible.

items.title: Python
items.description: Versatile, general-purpose language. I use it mostly for scripting and small data analysis, but it's also a guilty pleasure I enjoy coming back to every now and then.

items.title: Bash
items.description: The shell scripting language behind most Linux systems. I use it regularly to manage my servers, in CI/CD pipelines, and when tweaking my Linux workstations.

items.title: HTML & CSS
items.description: The building blocks of every web page. Working mainly in frontend development, I use HTML & CSS as part of my job every day.
